nodes.label: Seir
nodes.description: A Horite; one of the “dukes” of Edom (Gen. 36:20-30).

nodes.label: Amalek
nodes.description: Dweller in a valley, the son of Eliphaz and grandson of Esau (Gen. 36:12; 1 Chr. 1:36); the chief of an Idumean tribe (Gen. 36:16). His mother was a Horite, a tribe whose territory the descendants of Esau had seized.

nodes.label: Dishan
nodes.description: Antelope, the youngest son of Seir the Horite, head of one of the tribes of Idumaea (Gen. 36:21, 28, 30).

nodes.label: Ezer
nodes.description: One of the sons of Seir, the native princes, “dukes,” of Mount Hor (Gen. 36:21, 27).

nodes.label: Lotan
nodes.description: Coverer, one of the sons of Seir, the Horite (Gen. 36:20, 29).

nodes.label: Shobal
nodes.description: The second son of Seir the Horite; one of the Horite “dukes” (Gen. 36:20).

nodes.label: Eliphaz
nodes.description: The son of Esau by his wife Adah, and father of several Edomitish tribes (Gen. 36:4, 10, 11, 16).

verses.text: And the sons of Lotan; Hori, and Homam: and Timna was Lotan’s sister.
The sons of Eliphaz; Teman, and Omar, Zephi, and Gatam, Kenaz, and Timna, and Amalek.
And Timna was concubine to Eliphaz Esau’s son; and she bare to Eliphaz Amalek: these were the sons of Adah Esau’s wife.
